danger_signals.visual_form: Grid coordinates marked after successful hit
danger_signals.meaning: Player has located hostile vessel position

danger_signals.visual_form: Grid marker showing opponent's targeting attempt
danger_signals.meaning: Player's fleet is under threat at specific coordinates

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual elimination of ship sprite from player's fleet display
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of asset; reduced defensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Grid marker indicating no hit (typically blank or distinct color)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; no threat at that coordinate

safety_signals.visual_form: Ship sprite visible and undamaged in fleet display
safety_signals.meaning: Continued asset availability and defensive/offensive capacity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent fleet displayed on separate grid or screen
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Grid layout showing player-controlled ships; typically top or left portion of screen

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mirror grid display showing only hit/miss markers, not ship positions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Grid layout separated from player fleet; represents unknown enemy disposition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Static ship placement grid; player positioning vessels before engagement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turn-based pause state; no active targeting or movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both grids visible; marked coordinates showing hits/misses accumulating
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alternating turn execution; grid updates after each player action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual damage markers on ship sprites or grid positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced ship count or visible hit accumulation on vessels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent's entire fleet eliminated from grid display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game progression halts; victory screen or end-game state triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player's entire fleet eliminated from display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game progression halts; defeat screen or restart prompt

environmental_storytelling.example: Coordinate system (alphanumeric or numeric grid) communicates naval combat abstraction; encodes unknown threat geometry
Distinct markers (X for hit, O for miss, or color coding) convey success/failure of intelligence gathering about enemy position
Ship roster visible during gameplay indicates remaining assets and strategic options available to player
Sequential grid updates communicate turn economy and symmetry of advantage in naval engagement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remains visible on grid; functional for strategic defense
Visual marker applied (typically hit indicator); reduced or eliminated from active roster
No visual representation; coordinates unexplored on player's targeting grid
Grid coordinate marked as hit; enemy vessel confirmed at location
Complete grid area marked as destroyed; ship removed from enemy roster visibility

notes: Battleship NES (1993) is a digital adaptation of the classic guessing game paradigm. Visual communication is minimal and abstracted: the core manifestation system relies on grid-state (coordinates marked as hit/miss) and ship inventory visibility rather than cinematic or thematic visual styling. No faction identity exists beyond player vs. opponent symmetry. World state communicates through presence/absence of ship sprites and accumulation of hit markers. This is a pure puzzle/strategy game with no environmental storytelling or thematic visual language—communication occurs entirely through game-mechanic state visualization.
